The August FIDE rating list was published on August 1st, and Wikipedia updates its rankings page to match. Since the 2025-08-17 baseline ranking is 16, the 2025-08-24 ranking will almost certainly be the exact same list and value (thus 16, logically 'as high or higher' being true). For subsequent dates, new monthly FIDE lists will apply. While Kosteniuk remains an active and strong Top 20 player, she faces significant downward pressure from rising stars (such as Divya Deshmukh, who recently broke into the Top 15 following the World Cup). Consequently, as time goes on, the probability of her maintaining or improving upon the #16 ranking steadily decreases due to both normal rating variance and a slight general age-related decline paired with younger players' upward momentum. Uncertainty is accordingly centered closer to 0.5 for the distant dates.

FIDE ratings are updated on the 1st of each month. Therefore, on August 24, Harika Dronavalli's FIDE ranking will unquestionably be exactly what it was on August 7 (Rank #12). This makes resolution for the first date an almost certain YES (rank <= 12). For future months, we must anticipate natural rating fluctuations. As of the July/August rating lists, Dronavalli (#12) sits on a very slim margin above Mariya Muzychuk (#13), with only a 2-point rating difference (2488 vs 2486). Furthermore, younger, rapidly improving Indian prospects like 19-year-old Divya Deshmukh (who just won the 2025 Women's World Cup and jumped to #15) are actively gaining rating points and placing heavy downward pressure on the rankings of veteran players. Because of this, it is slightly more likely that Dronavalli will drift down to #13 or #14 than successfully push upward, as she needs to overcome a larger 14-point gap to overtake #11 Nana Dzagnidze. As the forecasting horizon extends into September, November, and next February, the probability of her effectively defending her #12 position slowly drops due to rating variance and the strength of rising juniors, anchoring towards 0.5 to accurately represent the increased uncertainty over time.

The base rate of vaccine development is very low, normally taking many years from initial research to clinical trials and approval. Wikipedia currently shows no vaccine for Colorado tick fever as of July 2025. A review of the literature up to early 2025 indicates that significant effort is still in the purely basic science stage (e.g., reverse genetics systems established in February 2025, in-silico "reverse vaccinology" design published in April 2024). There are no clinical trials underway for a CTF vaccine, nor is there any mention of a candidate entering advanced development. Given the timelines required, it is virtually impossible that a vaccine will be successfully developed and added to the relevant Wikipedia list by August to November 2025, or even February 2026. Therefore, the probability starts very low and only increases very slightly for the later dates to account for the infinitesimally small chance of a rapid off-label approval or error on Wikipedia.

The target threshold is the ADP market close on 2025-08-15, which was 295.86. Short-term (1-week and 1-month) momentum has been slightly negative over the past couple months after peaking at $318 in June, pushing initial probabilities below 0.50. Additionally, an upcoming ex-dividend date on September 12 (approx. $1.54/share) provides a slight mechanical downward bias against the September 16 resolution compared to a pure random walk. Over longer horizons (November, February), standard market drift (~8-10% annualized) and the company's strong fundamentals (recent earnings beats, raised revenue forecasts) dominate, moving the probability back above 0.50 and scaling upward with time.

AJG stock recently faced a significant drop from ~312 to the low 280s driven by lowered organic growth expectations and analyst downgrades before its Q2 earnings (Search_0_Result_6, Search_0_Result_8). Its 15% Q2 revenue growth report on July 31 has since provided minor support, contributing to a short-term rebound to 290.4. However, the 60-day linear trend remains negative, and the baseline statistical extrapolation from the TS tool predicts a 40-46% chance of staying above 290.42 in the short term. Weekly stock movements are very noisy, so I anchor close to 50% but apply a slight discount for the near term due to recent downward momentum and seasonal factors (P=0.44 for 2025-08-24 and P=0.47 for 2025-09-16). For the longer-term dates (2025-11-15 and 2026-02-13), the natural positive drift of equity markets and mean reversion pulls the probability back to 0.50 and slightly above (0.52).

BKNG recently reported strong Q2 2025 earnings resulting in a 10% dividend hike and the announcement of a large $20B share buyback program. After a 'sell-the-news' dip from a July high of ~227 down to ~213 (split-adjusted), the stock has started stabilizing around ~216.89 as of August 15. The linear projection from the past 3 months indicates a short-term upward drift (P > 216.9 is roughly 0.54). Given the strong buyback backstop and stabilized support, I estimate the short-term probability of the stock closing higher than the August 17 referent to be slightly above 50% (0.55). In strict accordance with the prompt's instruction to increase uncertainty and regress towards 0.50 as the horizon extends, my probabilities decrease sequentially for 1-month, 3-month, and 6-month horizons. (Note: The extreme price discrepancy found in the prompt's Aug 7 value vs YFinance's most recent prices is due to a recent stock split, which the resolution criteria explicitly accounts for without issue).

The stock price plunged sharply on July 31 following its Q2 earnings report, falling from around $293 down to $258 in early August. However, the drop appears to have been an unwarranted overreaction or tied to broader market volatility, as earnings actually beat expectations ($7.20 EPS vs $7.16 estimate) and revenues grew 11%. Analysts (e.g. at Wells Fargo) expressed surprise at the selloff, and the consensus price target remains around $379. Over the first half of August, the stock has shown intense positive momentum, recovering essentially all of its post-earnings losses to close at $292.11 on August 15. The statistical trend analysis from Yahoo Finance gives a combined probability of 0.56 for the near-term horizon based on this momentum and seasonal trends. I use 0.56 as my baseline for the 1-week forecast, and gracefully decay the probability toward 0.5 for the more distant horizons, per instructions.

The current price threshold is 427.9 (August 15, 2025 close). The short-to-medium-term momentum heavily slants downward from a peak of ~492 in July. Indicators for the short term include: 
1) A Zacks Rank of #4 (Sell).
2) The upcoming earnings report on August 27, where consensus expects a substantial 20% YoY decline in EPS ($0.83). 
3) Extremely high valuation (Forward P/E > 120) which leaves little room for earnings misses. 
4) Lingering concerns regarding the Delta lawsuit over the July 2024 IT global outage (which a judge allowed to proceed in May 2025) and softer sector guidance from peers like Fortinet. 
The statistical tool baseline P(increase) for 1-week forecast was exactly 0.44. Given these bearish headwinds, the probabilities for August and September remain below 0.5. For November and February resolution dates, uncertainty increases significantly, meaning probabilities must revert toward the general market's long-term historical upward drift (just above 0.50).

Thus, the probabilities for the subsequent resolution dates decay to 0.47 and 0.49.

DECK closed at 102.96 on August 15, 2025, which will serve as the benchmark price for August 17. The stock has experienced a sharp 50% year-to-date decline driven by broader macro weakness in the luxury/apparel sector and a projected $185 million COGS impact from rising Vietnam tariffs. However, it appears to have found strong technical support around the $100 level. In its recent Q1 FY2026 earnings on July 24, DECK reported an impressive beat with revenue up 17% and EPS up 24%. Furthermore, the board authorized a massive $2.25 billion stock repurchase program in May, which represents approximately 14-15% of the company's current market cap and provides a strong near-to-medium-term price floor. Statistical analysis provides a combined estimate of 0.58 for a near-term price increase over the 103 threshold, reflecting seasonal trends and linear stabilization. Therefore, the stock is slightly favored to rise from its current depressed state in the short term, but per instructions, the probabilities fade toward the base rate of 0.50 as the forecasting horizon extends.

The stock closed at a local peak of 177.39 on August 15, pushing it notably above its linear trend from May to August. Short-term mean reversion risks suggest the stock is somewhat more likely to fall than rise in the immediate term, aligning with the yfinance tool’s combined projected probability of 0.39. Following instructions to stay near 0.5 and increase uncertainty (move closer to 0.5) over longer horizons, the short-term estimates lean slightly bearish (0.45, 0.47). As the time horizon expands to 3 and 6 months, natural positive drift in equities begins to counterbalance technical mean reversion and occasional dividend payouts, bringing the probabilities strictly closer to 50/50 and slightly positive by early 2026 (0.50, 0.52).

The question asks whether DXCM will close higher than its Aug 15 close (80.95) on four future dates. The time-series analysis provides a combined estimate of P(> 80.95) = 0.42 based on a downward medium-term linear trend over the last 3 months, despite some recent upward momentum. As instructed, I use this 0.42 as my primary anchor for the shortest resolution date (1 week ahead: Aug 24). DXCM recently suffered a post-earnings drop in late July 2025 to ~75 and quickly rebounded to 80.95, increasing the likelihood of some near-term mean-reversion downward. Over longer horizons, the predictive power of current local momentum and specific seasonal signals naturally decays. Assuming stock prices basically follow a random walk with drift, the probabilities of being above the current price generally trend back to, and slightly above, 0.50 due to the equity market risk premium. Accordingly, the probability smoothly drifts from the 0.42 anchor back toward 0.50 for the longest horizon (February 2026).

The base rate probability anchor provided by the yfinance tool gives a combined estimate of 0.43 for the short-term forecast (August 24), primarily driven by unfavorable seasonal comparisons despite the positive linear trend. ETR's recent technicals show mixed signals, and importantly, it just passed its ex-dividend date on August 13 ($0.60 per share), which mechanically caused a minor drop to 87.95 on August 15 (the effective market close for August 17). Going forward, though long-term utility drifts are generally positive, the required confidence decay rule mandates that predicted probabilities move strictly towards 0.5 as the forecast horizon expands and uncertainty increases. Therefore, I anchor the 1-week forecast at 0.43, and incrementally shift the probability towards 0.50 for the subsequent dates spanning out to February 2026.

The stock is currently experiencing short-term negative downward momentum, having faded the July late-month earnings beat from a peak of ~102 back to 94. The statistical trend analysis gives a combined estimate of 0.41 for a 1-week horizon, supporting a near-term dip. However, over longer horizons (3-6 months), expanding into the fall and winter months, natural gas stocks typically exhibit favorable seasonality due to seasonal heating demand, pushing the probability of being higher closer to and slightly above 50% as noise and the general long-term equity premium factor in. As the largest natural gas producer in the US, EXE is highly exposed to this winter seasonality, supporting probabilities of 53% and 55% for November 2025 and February 2026.

The stock has recently bounced back from a bottom of around $134 up to $138.43 by August 15. There is solid support thanks to a large $2 billion share buyback program that actively bought back shares in the $133–$142 range over the last couple of quarters, acting as a soft price floor. The time-series analysis tool estimates a 53% probability of the stock moving upwards by late August based purely on recent linear drift (+0.04/day), and even higher based on annual cyclicality. The most recent Q2 dividend was expected to be paid August 14, meaning the stock already passed its ex-dividend date, so there isn't an immediate mechanical headwind for the late August and mid-September resolution dates. Following the explicit prompt instructions to let uncertainty strictly increase over the forecast horizon, I am scaling my estimates from an initially moderately confident 0.58 down towards 0.52 for the later dates where predictability decreases and random market fluctuations dominate.

The baseline threshold for resolution is $31.03 (the closing price on August 15, representing August 17). GEN recently announced Q1 2026 earnings on August 7, beating revenue expectations (up 30%) and raising annual guidance. This caused the stock to jump from around $27.87 to a peak of $31.71 on August 13. However, the stock has since pulled back over the last two trading sessions to $31.03, indicating short-term momentum is cooling off and mean-reverting post-earnings. Furthermore, GEN's Board announced a $0.125 cash dividend with a record date of August 18. This means the stock will go ex-dividend on Monday, mathematically dropping the opening price by $0.125 (lowering the effective start to ~30.90). To exceed 31.03 by August 24, the stock will need to overcome this ex-dividend gap and recent negative momentum, making the short-term probability less than 50% (~43%). For later dates, the strong underlying fundamentals from the guidance increase will battle against natural reversion and market volatility. The standard Black-Scholes estimate for a minor gap over time gradually drifts back toward a slightly positive coin toss. Adhering to the specific instruction that 'uncertainty should INCREASE with forecast horizon' and probabilities should be 'closer to 0.5' for distant dates, the estimates steadily regress toward 0.49.

The statistical trend gives a base rate of around ~0.46-0.56 for the short term, but the linear regression predicts a short-term downward drift to 46.48. Recent news is primarily negative regarding fundamentals: for Fiscal 2025 ending in May, Q4 earnings had a lackluster 2026 outlook and reported full-year North American retail sales dropped 5%. This drove the slide from ~$51 down to ~$47 over June and July. 
General Mills pays a substantial dividend (now $0.61 per quarter) with ex-dividend dates around the 10th of July, October, January, and April. An ex-dividend date occurs around mid-October, reducing the price by $0.61 in advance of the Nov 15 check, meaning the stock has to overcome this drop just to stay flat. Another ex-dividend in early January adds another -$0.61 hurdle ahead of the Feb 2026 resolution date.
Q1 2026 earnings should land roughly on September 17-18, likely after the Sept 16 date, making the short-term estimates rely more on random walk. Given the negative momentum, and the fundamental weakness projected for Fiscal 2026 (General Mills projected a "larger fiscal 2026 profit drop than analysts expected"), the drift over the next months looks slightly negative, with the scheduled dividend payouts mechanically subtracting from the price over the longer periods. As variance increases with longer horizons, the underlying mathematical trend should push longer-term horizons to ~0.50, but the steady -$0.61 per quarter dividend headwind explicitly reduces the predicted closing price. Therefore, probabilities tilt downwards progressively with time to account for dividends minus positive overall equity drift.

Given the negative momentum, and the fundamental weakness projected for Fiscal 2026 (General Mills projected a "larger fiscal 2026 profit drop than analysts expected"), the drift over the next months looks slightly negative, with the scheduled dividend payouts mechanically subtracting from the price over the longer periods. As variance increases with longer horizons, the underlying mathematical trend should push longer-term horizons to ~0.50, but the steady -$0.61 per quarter dividend headwind explicitly reduces the predicted closing price. Therefore, probabilities tilt downwards progressively with time to account for dividends minus positive overall equity drift.

Corning (GLW) recently saw a huge jump following an excellent Q2 2025 earnings report on July 29, reaching around $65 mid-August. The reference date's price is the August 15 close (around $64.83). While fundamentals seem to be improving with data center AI growth, current metrics show GLW is trading at a "full valuation" where the highest analyst target prices (like JP Morgan's) are pegged precisely at $65. Seeking Alpha just published a "Hold" rating on August 15, citing this peak and recommending an entry at a lower base. Furthermore, the Fetch_ts_yfinance gives an estimate of roughly 0.41 for a price higher than the $64.83 on August 24, as upside momentum appears capped and some slight mean reversion is quite possible. The ex-dividend date is also coming up around late August (for roughly $0.28), which will create structural downward pressure before the second resolution date (September 16). Given that the current price evaluates near the peak of consensus target ranges, the likelihood of GLW hovering around or slightly below the August 15 close price in the near term is higher than the likelihood of it breaking out higher, leaving P(higher) slightly under 50%. As we travel further into the future (Nov 2025 and Feb 2026), the usual stock drift upward and longer-term AI growth narrative provide a gentle tailwind, so I regress probabilities closer toward 0.5.

The yfinance tool indicates that GM's stock spiked recently, closing at 55.92 on Aug 15. The statistical analysis of the local trend projects a near-term value of roughly 54.94, suggesting this recent high is somewhat extended, meaning a slight near-term pullback is more likely than continued immediate gains. Consequently, the initial probability estimate for staying above 55.92 over the next week is below 50% (0.44). As the forecast horizon extends to 1, 3, and 6 months out, the variance of outcomes rapidly increases, and stock movements essentially approximate a random walk. Coupled with the standard equity premium (which imparts a slight upward bias over long horizons), this mounting uncertainty drives the estimates gradually closer to 0.5. Expected dividend payouts throughout late 2025 and 2026 also generate minor headwinds to absolute price levels, keeping even the distant probabilities firmly anchored near a coin flip.

Hologic recently beat its Q3 2025 earnings estimates (reporting $1.08 EPS vs $1.05 expected), providing solid fundamental support for the stock's recent climb from $64.98 to a peak of $68.98 in mid-August, before settling at $67.81 on August 15. The linear model from the fetched Yfinance data evaluates the baseline probability of an initial short-term rise at 0.52, with combined estimates up to 0.59. More importantly, the stock remains buoyed by persistent M&A speculation after the company rejected a $16 billion take-private proposal from TPG and Blackstone in May 2025, which would have valued the shares between $70 and $72. This creates an asymmetric near-term upside if revised bids emerge. However, given recent downward weekly momentum (pulling back from $68.98) and standard forecasting protocols dictating that uncertainty should increase over longer horizons, the initial positive skew is incrementally pulled back towards 0.50 (a pure random walk) for the distant resolution dates as current catalysts gradually lose predictive power.

The reference price for this question will be the Friday, August 15, 2025 closing price (since Aug 17 is a Sunday). On August 15, evidence shows IPG stock experienced a very sharp single-day spike of about 6.32% along with a 5.91% jump in Omnicom (OMC) stock, despite both stocks previously trending downward over the last month. Since IPG is being acquired by OMC at a fixed exchange ratio of 0.344 shares (expected to close H2 2025), IPG tracks OMC closely. Starting the forecast from an artificially inflated post-spike base price implies a lower probability of the stock moving even higher in the immediate short-term due to mean reversion (hangover effect). Accordingly, the 1-week forecast (Aug 24) is below 50%. As we project further out to Sept 16 and Nov 15, natural positive stock drift offsets the tough base-price baseline, and the probability reverts toward 0.50. The minimal risk of the deal breaking (which would be catastrophic for the stock price) adds a slight negative skew, providing further justification for keeping the long-term estimate slightly under 0.50.

The stock experienced a significant ~15% drop following its Q2 2025 earnings report on July 31, falling from ~$52 to ~$45. This was likely driven by integration costs and one-time charges related to its recent £5.8B acquisition of DS Smith, despite reporting strong 28% top-line revenue growth. Following the initial post-earnings drop, the stock has stabilized in the $44-$47 range during the first half of August, showing some signs of consolidation. The historical time series tool projects a short-term probability of an increase around 54% based on recent linear trends and historical seasonality. Short-term momentum could drive a slight bounce back as the market digests the earnings reaction. However, over the longer term, there remain key uncertainties regarding the execution of the DS Smith integration, realized synergies, and potential margin pressures as long-term volume demand has remained stagnant over a five-year horizon. Following guidelines that state uncertainty should increase with forecast horizon, the longer-term estimates gradually regress toward 0.50, reflecting the difficulty in predicting specific market outcomes over longer timeframes amidst these acquisition-related structural shifts.

The stock market closing price of KDP on 2025-08-15 (which acts as the 2025-08-17 weekend price) reached 33.89, representing a local peak in its recent trading range of $29.50 – $35.00. The rapid gain following strong Q2 sentiment pushes the current price above its immediate linear trend. Statistical tools estimate a ~40-46% chance of remaining above 33.89 in the short term (1 week out) due to likely mean reversion. For the first two dates, probabilities are set below 0.5 (0.43, 0.46). For the more distant dates, general positive market drift, bullish analyst targets ($\$40-\$48$), and the reduction of the JAB Holding share overhang gradually shift the expectation upward, pushing the probability toward and slightly above 0.5 (0.49, 0.52).

KMB saw an earnings-related price spike in early August (peaking around Aug 7) but has exhibited strong downward momentum over the past week, unwinding the pop back toward its pre-earnings baseline amid analysts slashing 2025 revenue estimates by 12-16% due to divestitures. This creates a strong near-term headwind, bringing the Aug 24 probability under 0.50. Furthermore, an upcoming $1.26 quarterly dividend with an ex-dividend date of Sept 5, 2025 will mechanically drop the unadjusted stock price by roughly 1%, creating a structural headwind for the Sep 16 and subsequent resolution dates. Longer-term, analysts maintain 12-month price targets around $141-$142, and general market drift tends to be positive, which slowly pulls the probabilities on the 3-month and 6-month horizons closer to 0.50 due to increased uncertainty and time for the stock to rebound.

The statistical tool estimate for the near-term probability of Kinder Morgan (KMI) exceeding its current threshold (26.118 on August 15) is 0.43, largely due to recent downward momentum since early August when the stock traded around 27.5. For the short-term forecast (August 24), this downward momentum and possible technical weakness keeps the probability slightly below 50%. Over longer horizons, the memory of this short-term trend decays and broader equity risk premiums (positive drift) start dominating, although high dividend-paying stocks like KMI experience regular price drops on ex-dividend dates (late Oct, late Jan) which partially offset capital appreciation. Consequently, the estimates gradually drift upward toward 0.5 and slightly beyond for the late 2025 and 2026 resolutions, as the baseline price of 26.12 is slightly below its recent average of ~26.5 from the past 3 months.

The reference price (closing price prior to Sunday, August 17, 2025) is $699.03, recorded on Friday, August 15. LLY experienced a dramatic 14% drop on August 7 due to disappointing pipeline data for an oral weight-loss drug, which pushed shares down to the $620-$630 range. However, this overshadows the fact that the company actually beat Q2 earnings estimates and raised its forward guidance due to robust demand for its approved blockbuster drugs. Recently, massive insider buying (including the CEO) and double upgrades from Wall Street analysts have catalyzed a strong four-day winning streak where the stock recovered from $622 to $699. Short-term momentum is solidly bullish, and the statistical trend analysis provided a combined probability estimate of 0.60 for the short-term horizon. I anchor my initial one-week probability at 0.61 based on this strong signal. Following explicit instructions, uncertainty over the long term increases (as market noise overcomes current momentum catalysts), meaning probabilities for more distant dates must scale down closer to the 0.5 base rate.

Recently, massive insider buying (including the CEO) and double upgrades from Wall Street analysts have catalyzed a strong four-day winning streak where the stock recovered from $622 to $699. Short-term momentum is solidly bullish, and the statistical trend analysis provided a combined probability estimate of 0.60 for the short-term horizon. I anchor my initial one-week probability at 0.61 based on this strong signal. Following explicit instructions, uncertainty over the long term increases (as market noise overcomes current momentum catalysts), meaning probabilities for more distant dates must scale down closer to the 0.5 base rate.

The stock price has recently shown positive short-term momentum, bouncing from roughly $50.72 on August 6 to $53.38 on August 15. This recovery has been supported by positive macroeconomic news (favorable CPI inflation data) and favorable corporate announcements, including a new "Focus to Win" cost-cutting program and management confidence (the Board electing to receive retainers in stock). Because of this near-term upward momentum and a lack of immediate negative catalysts before August 24, the short-term probability is estimated slightly above 50%. As per the instructions, uncertainty must increase with the forecast horizon, so the probabilities progressively smoothly converge toward 0.50. Over the 3- and 6-month horizons, intermediate quarterly earnings releases (likely in October and January) will introduce unforecastable variance, justifying a return to a near random-walk baseline (0.50-0.51). Furthermore, the dividend payable in late August already had its ex-dividend date on July 31, ensuring no artificial price drop before the September date, though subsequent ex-dividend dates in late October/early November provide a very minor downward technical pricing pressure for the later resolution dates.

MOS stock suffered an 11% drop on August 6 due to a Q2 earnings miss, landing around $30.18 before recovering slightly to the threshold value of $31.69 on August 15. The stock price currently reflects a tug-of-war between the negative momentum of the recent earnings miss and management's optimistic guidance for higher H2 2025 EBITDA and higher potash production. The near-term technicals (recovering from oversold RSI) are mixed, while the yfinance linear/seasonal estimate sets a slightly bearish prior (0.44). I assign a near-term probability slightly below 0.5 (0.47) to reflect the downside momentum and negative seasonality. As the forecast horizon extends to Q3 and Q4 2025, where management's stated EBITDA expansion is expected to materialize, fundamentally positive drift takes precedence and probability converges back to 0.50, abiding by the instruction prioritizing closer-to-0.5 predictions for distant dates.

Given that updated guidance from May was in-line to slightly above consensus (EPS $2.55-2.75) and analyst price targets generally range from $219 to $260 (both above the current price), there is a slight fundamental bias towards the upside, reinforcing the base-rate equity drift. OMC recently jumped from ~$71 to ~$74.15 on news that its $13.25B stock-for-stock acquisition of IPG (announced Dec 2024) cleared major regulatory hurdles and is launching exchange offers. The market close price on 2025-08-17 will be anchored to August 15's close of 74.15. The linear/statistical analysis gives a roughly 57% chance of upward continuation in the very short term, supported by recent positive momentum. However, acquirer stocks often face downward pressure around the close of all-stock deals due to arbitrage and dilution. Furthermore, OMC typically pays quarterly dividends in October and January, which will incrementally reduce the share price for the later resolution dates (November and February), offsetting the expected equity risk premium. Given these competing factors—short-term momentum vs. intermediate-term M&A indigestion and dividend drops—I predict probabilities slightly above 50% for the near term, gradually regressing toward 51-52% for the longer dates as uncertainty and dividend impacts weigh on the nominal stock price.

Given these competing factors—short-term momentum vs. intermediate-term M&A indigestion and dividend drops—I predict probabilities slightly above 50% for the near term, gradually regressing toward 51-52% for the longer dates as uncertainty and dividend impacts weigh on the nominal stock price.

POOL closed at 318.27 on August 15, 2025 (which serves as the benchmark for Sunday, Aug 17). The stock recently spiked from 308 to 326 on August 13 (likely front-running Berkshire Hathaway's 13F filing which revealed a 1.99M share purchase), but it has faced heavy selling pressure over the last two days, dropping to 322 and then 318.27, combined with an ex-dividend date on Aug 14. 

For the short-term resolutions (Aug 24, Sep 16), the strong downside momentum of this "sell the news" gap-fill means the stock is likely to retrace closer to the 310-315 range, keeping it below the 318.27 baseline. Therefore, the probabilities are set below 0.5. 
In the medium term (Nov 15), seasonal strength, broad equity market beta, and the fundamental floor provided by Berkshire Hathaway's significant buy-in should help the stock recover above 318.27, bringing the probability above 0.5. 
In the long term (Feb 13, 2026), forecasting confidence inherently decreases, and conforming to the instruction to increase uncertainty with horizon, the probability shifts closer to the 0.5 base rate while still maintaining a slight positive edge due to historical stock drift.

Stock price movements roughly follow a random walk with a modest positive drift over time due to the equity risk premium. For the shortest horizon (1 week), the fundamental drift combined with general noise centers the estimate slightly above 50% (0.52). 

Crucially, Stanley Black & Decker (SWK) pays a regular quarterly dividend of $0.82. Based on their historical schedule (e.g., June 3 for Q2), the next ex-dividend date will fall in early September (prior to the 2025-09-16 resolution). This mechanical price drop of roughly 1.2% on a $68 stock outpaces the expected monthly equity drift (estimated at ~0.8%), meaning the stock is more likely to be down than up from today's price by September 16. Thus, the 1-month probability drops below 50% (0.485). 

For the 3-month and 6-month horizons, further equity drift begins to catch up with and compensate for the dividend drops, moving the baseline expectation back into slightly positive territory. However, adhering strictly to the prompt's instruction ("Your uncertainty should INCREASE with forecast horizon — probabilities for distant dates should be closer to 0.5"), we shrink these longer-term probabilities toward exactly 50%. This yields a monotonically decaying distance from 0.5 (distances: 0.020, 0.015, 0.010, 0.005).

On August 11, 2025, Bloomberg reported that TransDigm launched a $4 billion debt deal to fund a $4.3 billion special cash dividend. With ~57 million shares, this will result in a special dividend of roughly $75 per share. Resolution relies strictly on unadjusted market close prices (no adjustment for dividends, only splits). Once the dividend goes ex-date, the stock will mechanically drop by ~$75. 

Using the 2024 timeline as a reference (debt Sept 3, declared Sept 20, ex-date Oct 4), we expect the ex-date to be around mid-September 2025 (e.g., ~Sept 11).
- 2025-08-24: Will resolve before the ex-dividend date. Expecting a normal short-term drift but pressured by recent negative earnings momentum. (0.46)
- 2025-09-16: High probability the stock has just gone ex-dividend, causing a mechanical $75 drop. It will be very hard to recover this in a short time. (0.28)
- 2025-11-15: The ex-dividend will have definitively occurred. The stock will be battling the $75 handicap but has ~2 months of expected upward drift to partially offset it. (0.39)
- 2026-02-13: After 6 months, standard expected stock market returns should roughly equal the $75 drop, neutralizing the handicap and centering the probability again. (0.49)

Using the 2024 timeline as a reference (debt Sept 3, declared Sept 20, ex-date Oct 4), we expect the ex-date to be around mid-September 2025 (e.g., ~Sept 11).
- 2025-08-24: Will resolve before the ex-dividend date. Expecting a normal short-term drift but pressured by recent negative earnings momentum. (0.46)
- 2025-09-16: High probability the stock has just gone ex-dividend, causing a mechanical $75 drop. It will be very hard to recover this in a short time. (0.28)
- 2025-11-15: The ex-dividend will have definitively occurred. The stock will be battling the $75 handicap but has ~2 months of expected upward drift to partially offset it. (0.39)
- 2026-02-13: After 6 months, standard expected stock market returns should roughly equal the $75 drop, neutralizing the handicap and centering the probability again. (0.49)

The stock (TECH) recently experienced a sharp post-earnings dip starting August 6, bottoming around $49.66 before rebounding to the baseline price of $53.38 by August 15. The earnings report was actually a slight beat (adjusted EPS of $0.53 vs. $0.50 expected) but weak forward guidance ('low single digits') caused initial volatility. Buying support quickly pushed the price back to pre-earnings levels. The linear and seasonal models from the automated statistical analysis suggest a moderately bullish short-term bias (P=0.60 prior) given the recent oversold bounce. Following the specific instruction that uncertainty must increase for distant dates, and recognizing that stock prices approximate a random walk over these horizons, I rate the nearest date highest due to short-term momentum from the earnings recovery, and then step the probabilities smoothly down toward 0.51 for the 6-month horizon as predictive power fades.

The baseline price for this forecast is the last market close before August 17, which was 99.74 on Friday, August 15. Tapestry (TPR) experienced a massive 15% drop on August 14 (from 112.40 to 94.74) following an earnings miss and a drastic cut to its profit forecast due to an expected $160 million hit from increased tariffs. The stock sharply initially rebounded an extra 5% to 99.74 on Friday, generating a mixed technical momentum picture. Given the concept of post-earnings announcement drift, negative sentiment, and the yfinance combined statistical estimate of 0.44, the near-term probability of staying above 99.74 is slightly unfavorable. However, historical stock market behavior follows a random walk with a long-term upward drift. As the forecast horizon increases to 3 and 6 months, mean reversion and general market drift make it more likely to steadily recover, gradually pushing the probability up to and slightly above 0.50.

The yfinance tool provided a base rate anchor of P(increase) = 0.55, which incorporates historical trends and recent momentum. Therefore, the probabilities steadily decrease toward maximum uncertainty (0.50) as the resolution date moves further out (from 1 week to 6 months).

The reference price on Aug 15 is 191.60. The stock has just experienced strong positive momentum, jumping from ~181 to 191.60 over the last 5 days following a Bernstein analyst report (Aug 12) noting upcoming price hikes intended to boost margins. Crucially, the stock held and added to the gains on Wednesday, Thursday, and Friday, showing sustained buying pressure rather than an immediate top. The yfinance tool gives a combined estimate of 0.59 for exceeding this threshold based on momentum and seasonal data. I use this as my anchor for the short term, putting the 1-week probability at 0.56. Following the prompt's explicit instruction that uncertainty should increase with forecast horizon (meaning probabilities for distant dates should converge toward 0.5), I gradually decay the momentum effect. Over longer horizons, this idiosyncratic price shock fades into general market noise, pulling the probability closer to 50/50. Therefore, the probabilities decrease smoothly from 0.56 down to 0.51 over the 6-month horizon.

Waste Management (WM) stock is at 224.20 on August 15, 2025. Over the last week, it has declined by roughly 4%, falling from ~234 to 224. The YFinance model predicts a 0.42 P(exceed) based on a moderate downward trend recently. Stock paths generally have slight positive drift, which usually supports going over 0.5 for long-term horizons, but the strong short-term momentum affects the first resolution date. Analysts are bullish on the name overall, keeping a target over $250.

For the August 24 resolution date (one week away), I am anchoring near 0.46. Downward momentum could easily drive it below the 224 threshold, but slight mean reversion might bump it up. The dividend dates are predicted to be early September (~Sep 5) which would reduce the price mechanically by ~0.825 on Ex-Date. Therefore, the September 16 resolution date would eat this mechanical drop. However, four weeks out gives more time to grow, so I'll put it at 0.48.

For November 15 (3 months out) and February 13, 2026 (6 months out), the positive drift of the stock has time to assert itself, overcoming the mechanical dip of one or two dividends. I therefore bring the probabilities closer to 0.50 and 0.51 respectively, matching a more typical random walk outcome combined with analyst optimism.

Therefore, the September 16 resolution date would eat this mechanical drop. However, four weeks out gives more time to grow, so I'll put it at 0.48.

For November 15 (3 months out) and February 13, 2026 (6 months out), the positive drift of the stock has time to assert itself, overcoming the mechanical dip of one or two dividends. I therefore bring the probabilities closer to 0.50 and 0.51 respectively, matching a more typical random walk outcome combined with analyst optimism.
